Maple Ridge Rehabilitation and Nursing

CCN: 46A058 · Salt Lake City, UT 84102 · Salt Lake County

Overview

Address
455 South 900 East, Salt Lake City, UT 84102
Phone
8013556891
Certified beds
36
Avg daily residents
34 (95% of beds filled)
Ownership
Government (city/county)
Provider type
Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1991-11-01
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 2.30 4.00 2.22 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.60 0.70 0.58 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.26 0.89 0.25 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.45 2.41 1.40 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 0.85 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.20
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.65
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.12
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
72.4%
annual
RN turnover
87.5%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.42
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.03
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-06-02 14 14 2 72 1 72
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-09-13 4 2 2 40 1 40
Total weighted health score
64.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (34)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

31 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0775 Keep complete, dated laboratory records in the resident's record. E 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-06-02 2025-06-27
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. E 2024-03-05 2024-03-20
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-03-05 2024-03-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2023-09-13 2023-10-20
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. G 2023-09-13 2023-10-20
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. E 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. E 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2021-11-15 2022-01-14
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2021-11-15 2022-01-14

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
